name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store chain with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed on both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating significant scale and institutional recognition in the consumer finance sector.

FirstCash's core business model centers on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers through pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property. Customers can also buy and sell a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. The company offers layaway services with 10% down payment options and buys precious metals (gold, silver, platinum) for immediate cash. Additionally, FirstCash operates AFF, a wholly-owned subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through over 15,000 retail merchant partner locations nationwide.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its massive geographic footprint and dual focus on both pawn lending and merchandise retail. Unlike smaller regional pawn shops, FirstCash combines traditional pawn operations with technology-driven point-of-sale payment solutions and an extensive lease-to-own network through AFF. The company's scale, public market listing, and diversified service offerings position it as a comprehensive alternative lending platform rather than a single-service operator.

FirstCash is best suited for consumers needing immediate cash without credit checks or underwriting, those looking to buy discounted merchandise, or individuals seeking to sell valuables quickly. However, pawn loans require surrendering collateral and come with interest rates and storage fees. The lease-to-own model through AFF can result in paying significantly more than retail prices over time. Consumers should understand that non-recourse pawn loans do not build credit history.
pros: Massive network of 3,300+ locations providing convenient access across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America
No credit check or approval process required for pawn loans—funds available immediately
Buys precious metals (gold, silver, platinum) for instant cash without waiting period
Non-recourse pawn loans mean no personal liability if unable to reclaim pledged item
Layaway service with only 10% down payment option for retail purchases
Publicly traded company (listed on S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000) indicating transparency and regulatory oversight
Wide variety of merchandise for purchase including electronics, tools, jewelry, and musical instruments
cons: Pawn loans require surrendering personal property as collateral, creating risk of losing items if unable to repay
Does not report pawn loan activity to credit bureaus, so loans do not build credit history
Interest rates and storage fees on pawn loans can be substantial and vary by location and loan duration
Lease-to-own through AFF subsidiary can result in paying 2-3x the retail price over the lease period
Merchandise inventory varies by location and can be unpredictable for specific items
best_for: Consumers with poor credit or no credit history needing immediate emergency cash without underwriting
Individuals looking to quickly convert valuables (jewelry, electronics, tools) into cash
Shoppers seeking discounted merchandise or unique items from pawn inventory
People seeking short-term loans without a traditional banking relationship
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or credit-constrained consumers needing immediate cash without creditworthiness verification, those with valuable items to sell, or bargain shoppers. The critical caveat is that pawn loans don't build credit, require surrendering collateral with risk of loss, and come with fees and interest that can accumulate quickly—making them expensive short-term financing compared to payday alternatives or personal loans.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property pledges
Gold and precious metals buying (gold, silver, platinum)
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Store locator and inventory search tools
Lease-to-own financing through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ retail partner locations)
In-store cash transactions for merchandise purchases
